# Column-header DnD resort misbehaves in locked multi-region grids

## Context

Operator friction report (2026-06-11 night shift, v13 cut window): with locked columns active in the devindex app, **column-header drag-and-drop to resort columns does not work right**. This is not a surprise regression ‚Äî it is the **declared L2‚ÜíL4 evidence gap of merged PR #12792 materializing as a live defect**. That PR's own Evidence line said: *"the cross-toolbar drag motion + within-region positioning in a locked-multi-region grid need a live-env visual-verify (operator)"* ‚Äî and its Post-Merge Validation carried an unchecked pre-ready visual-verify gate. The operator's devindex testing is that verification finally happening, and it fails.

`#9491` (the SortZone cross-header DnD overhaul) closed COMPLETED via PR #12792 on 2026-06-09, so the live misbehavior currently has **no open ticket home**. This ticket is that home.

## The Problem

Symptom as reported: header-drag column resort misbehaves when the grid has locked regions (devindex: 3√ó `locked: 'start'` + 1√ó `locked: 'end'`). The precise failure modes (wrong drop position within a region? wrong region resolution? index drift after cross-region re-home? motion/feedback desync?) are **not yet characterized** ‚Äî characterization is AC1, because the sanctioned e2e path cannot simulate pointer-driven column drags (that is exactly why #12807 exists; PR #12792 could only unit-test the pure helpers).

Repro surface: `apps/devindex/view/home/GridContainer.mjs` ‚Äî columns `#`/`rank`/`login` (`locked: 'start'`, ~L195/201/209) + `lastUpdated` (`locked: 'end'`, ~L366). Drag column headers to resort, within and across regions.

## The Architectural Reality

- `src/draggable/grid/header/toolbar/SortZone.mjs` ‚Äî PR #12792 shipped the functional core: `getDropRegion` (drop-region detection), `columnIndexOffset` (locked-region index offset), `onDragEnd` integration (cross-region `column.locked` override + within-region `moveTo` offset).
- Evidence ceiling at merge was **L2**: `test/playwright/unit/draggable/grid/header/toolbar/SortZone.spec.mjs` covers the pure helpers; the `onDragEnd` integration is embedded in an async method and was explicitly not end-to-end verified.
- #12807 (open) is the missing verification substrate: Mouse.mjs-sensor column-DnD is not whitebox-e2e-simulatable; until it lands, any fix here re-ships at the same L2 ceiling unless a documented manual L3 protocol is executed.
- The wider multi-body architecture lane (#9872 3-tier orchestration, #9492 split-row selection, umbrella epic #9486) continues post-v13 per the v13 release-note grid cut-line; this ticket is the narrow defect leaf, not the architecture lane.

## The Fix

1. **Characterize first** (AC1): build the misbehavior matrix on devindex ‚Äî drag within `start` region, within `center`, within `end`, and across each region boundary (both directions); record expected vs actual landing index/region per case.
2. Fix the identified defect(s) in `SortZone.mjs` (`onDragEnd` within-region positioning and/or `columnIndexOffset` semantics), keeping the shipped pure-helper contracts where they verify correct.
3. Verify at the highest reachable level: through #12807's simulation substrate if landed; otherwise a documented manual L3 visual protocol with the matrix from AC1 ‚Äî do NOT close at L2 again.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: Misbehavior matrix documented on this ticket (case √ó expected √ó actual), reproduced on devindex with locked `start` + `end` regions
- [ ] AC2: Resort lands columns at the correct index in the correct region for every matrix case
- [ ] AC3: Regression coverage at L3+ (via #12807 substrate, or documented manual protocol executed and recorded) ‚Äî closing at L2-only is explicitly forbidden
- [ ] AC4: Disposition recorded for PR #12792's promised-but-unfiled "cross-boundary visual indicators" follow-up (file it or fold-with-rationale at pickup; it belongs to the original author's lane by default)

## Out of Scope

- Removing the `locked` configs from devindex for the v13 window ‚Äî that is a separate operator release-optics call (4-line config change), not part of the defect fix.
- The #9872 3-tier orchestration refactor and #9492 selection-model work ‚Äî architecture lane, post-v13.
- The cross-boundary drag-feedback visual indicators themselves (polish; AC4 only records their disposition).

## Avoided Traps

- **Re-shipping at L2** ‚Äî the root failure pattern of this defect's lineage; AC3 makes the evidence floor explicit.
- **Folding the indicators polish into the defect fix** ‚Äî #12792 deliberately deferred them as polish to be shaped against verified motion; verified motion does not exist yet.
- **Treating this as the architecture lane** ‚Äî the fix targets the shipped functional core's behavior, not the View-owned orchestration end-state.

## Decision Record impact

none.

Release classification: boardless ‚Äî post-v13 (v13 grid cut-line scopes multi-body completion post-release; the v13 note claims foundation + region *resolution* only, which holds).

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ‚ùã **Author refinement ‚Äî agent-executable verification path (operator framing: #12807 + whitebox-e2e = agents can "see" and debug the drag).**
> 
> Sharpening AC1/AC3 with the concrete mechanism, per `.agents/skills/whitebox-e2e/references/whitebox-e2e-protocol.md` + ada's live-probe correction on #12807 (2026-06-10):
>

Drive each matrix case with the corrected dispatch sequence from #12807 ‚Äî `mousedown(clientX:X) ‚Üí wait ‚â•100ms (Mouse.mjs:81 delay gate) ‚Üí mousemove(clientX:X+Œî, Œî‚â•5) ‚Üí mouseup` ‚Äî then assert **engine truth**, not DOM: `nlApp.queryComponent` on the header toolbars / grid container for column order, `locked` region membership, and indices inside the App Worker. Expected-vs-actual per case IS the matrix.
> - **AC3 evidence floor:** if #12807's sequence verifies, AC3's "L3+ regression coverage" lands as exactly that spec, runnable via `npx playwright test test/playwright/e2e/<spec> -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs` (never the default config). The manual-protocol fallback remains only for the case where #12807's verification refutes the test-side hypothesis.

> Dependency stays soft (prose, not `blocked_by`): #12807 is the preferred vehicle, not a hard gate.

- 2026-06-10T23:33:28Z @neo-fable assigned to @neo-fable
- 2026-06-10T23:33:58Z @neo-fable cross-referenced by #12696
### @neo-fable - 2026-06-10T23:55:45Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ‚ùã ## AC1 characterization matrix ‚Äî live run, 2026-06-11 night (operator override: pre-cut lane)
> 
> **Rig:** devindex (50,000 rows) in an agent-managed preview browser, 1680√ó1000, dev mode, fresh session. Assertion plane = Neural Link (vdom child order + DOM rects + console + DragCoordinator state); dispatch plane = in-page `MouseEvent` sequences (see #12807 note below for why not `simulate_event`). Baseline regions: **start** `[#, Rank, User]` x17‚Äì387 ¬∑ **center** `[Total, Commits %, Private %, Impact, Activity, Top Repo, ‚Ä¶, 2010]` x387‚Äì1543 ¬∑ **end** `[Updated]` x1543‚Äì1663.
> 
> **Dispatch sequence (worked on first attempt ‚Äî `#12807`'s corrected hypothesis):** `mousedown(clientX, clientY, buttons:1)` on the header button ‚Üí 150ms wait ‚Üí 4√ó `mousemove` on `document` (first Œî=10px) at ~60ms cadence ‚Üí `mouseup(buttons:0)`. Sensor armed, SortZone executed, zero console errors, DragCoordinator clean (`{}`) after drop.
> 
> ### Case 1 ‚Äî within-center drag
> 
> | | |
> |---|---|
> | Action | Drag `Total` (center idx 0, from x437) ‚Üí drop **x585** (inside `Private %`) |
> | Expected | `Total` ‚Üí center idx 1‚Äì2 |
> | Actual (engine, vdom-verified) | `Total` ‚Üí **center idx 5** (after `Top Repo`) ‚Äî overshoot +3‚Äì4 ¬∑ **AND start region reordered to `[Rank, User, #]`** (untouched region mutated) |
> 
> **Numeric fit:** drop x585 ‚àí grid-left 17 = 568 ‚Üí cumulative *grid-global* widths (60, 60, 250, 100, 90, 90‚Ä¶) place 568 at **global index 5** ‚Üí that global index applied **region-relatively** in center (`Total` ‚Üí idx 5 ‚úì exact) AND in start (drag-source `indexOf` = ‚àí1 ‚Üí from 0; to 5 clamped to 2 ‚Üí `#` ‚Üí idx 2 ‚úì exact).
> 
> ### Case 2 ‚Äî within-start drag (H2 discriminator)
> 
> | | |
> |---|---|
> | Action | Drag `Rank` (start idx 0, from x47) ‚Üí drop **x350** (inside `#`, still within start bounds) |
> | Expected | `Rank` ‚Üí start idx 2; no other region touched |
> | Actual (engine, vdom-verified) | **`#` EJECTED from start into center idx 0** (start itemCount 3‚Üí2 `[User, Rank]`, center 33‚Üí34) ‚Äî locked-region **membership corruption from a same-region drag** |
> 
> ### Findings
> 
> 1. **F1 ‚Äî wrong index space:** within-region drop index is computed in grid-global coordinate space and applied region-relatively (case 1 fits to the pixel). The L2-tested helpers may each be correct in isolation; the composition in `onDragEnd` mixes spaces.
> 2. **F2 ‚Äî no drag-ownership guard:** all three per-toolbar SortZones (`neo-grid-header-toolbar-sortzone-1/2/3` confirmed live) consume the same document-level drag. A drag started in toolbar A executes moves in toolbars B/C with `from = indexOf(missing source) ‚Üí 0` and a clamped target.
> 3. **F3 ‚Äî membership corruption:** a within-start drag triggered a cross-region re-home of a *non-dragged* column (`#` ‚Üí center idx 0), i.e. the cross-region branch can fire in a non-owning zone and mutate `column.locked` membership.
> 4. **F4 (bonus, narrow-viewport):** at 424px width the center region collapses to 0px and the end toolbar overflows the grid container's right edge (x387‚Äì507 vs container edge 408) ‚Äî region width clamping gap, separate from DnD.
> 5. Body cells follow the corrupted header order (screenshots captured); no exceptions thrown anywhere ‚Äî the corruption is silent.
> 
> ### Fix shape (for the implementation pass)
> 
> 1. Ownership guard: only the SortZone whose toolbar contains the drag-source element processes the drag end-to-end.
> 2. Normalize drop-x to the **owning region's origin** before index computation (`columnIndexOffset` composition).
> 3. Cross-region re-home mutates `column.locked` exactly once, in the owning zone, for the **dragged** column only.
> 4. Regression specs per matrix case using the confirmed dispatch sequence (#12807 vehicle); matrix cases 3‚Äì5 (intentional cross-region: center‚Üístart, center‚Üíend, end‚Üícenter) run post-fix or per-case with fresh reloads ‚Äî case 2's membership corruption invalidates same-session continuation.
> 
> AC1: cases 1‚Äì2 done with mechanism hypothesis + exact numeric fits; cases 3‚Äì5 deferred to the fix pass. AC2/AC3 next: read `SortZone.onDragEnd` + `getDropRegion` + `columnIndexOffset` source against F1‚ÄìF3, implement, verify with this rig.

- 2026-06-11T00:17:03Z @neo-fable cross-referenced by #12880
- 2026-06-11T00:18:03Z @neo-fable referenced in commit `386c589` - "fix(grid): resolve dragged column identity in SortZone onDragEnd (#12878)"
### @neo-fable - 2026-06-11T00:18:31Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ‚ùã ## Fix implemented + live-verified; AC2 scope amendment (author)
> 
> **Three root causes fixed in `src/draggable/grid/header/toolbar/SortZone.mjs`** (branch `agent/12878-sortzone-locked-resort`, commit `386c589db`):
> 
> 1. **Stale/local index against the global collection (F1/F3 root):** `onDragEnd` derived `toIndex` from the dragged element's `aria-colindex` ‚Äî toolbar-local AND pre-drag-stale ‚Äî and indexed the global `columns` collection with it, so the lock-inference inspected and **mutated an unrelated column** (case 1: it unlocked `#` instead of touching `Total`). Fix: capture the dragged column's `dataField` at drag-start; re-resolve column + global index from the collection AFTER the move applies; never run lock-logic on an unresolvable column.
> 2. **Only-override-on-difference positional check:** the release-x region check only overrode the neighbor inference when the region differed from the column's current one ‚Äî so within-region drops at a locked region's inner edge fell through to the inference's `null` and got silently ejected. Fix: when a release coordinate exists, the positional region IS the lock verdict for every drop; the neighbor inference remains only as the no-coordinate fallback.
> 3. **Drag-end rects race the mid-drag re-flow:** hiding the dragged column's cells re-flows the locked bodies, so the drag-end body-rect read placed an in-region release (x350) outside its own shrunken region (`bodyStart` 370px‚Üí310px) ‚Üí region `null` ‚Üí ejection. Fix: snapshot the region rects at drag-START (the geometry the user drops against) and resolve against the snapshot.
> 
> **Post-fix verification (fresh sessions, same rig + dispatch protocol as the characterization):**
> 
> | Case | Result |
> |---|---|
> | 2 ‚Äî `Rank` within-start (x107‚Üí350, the ejection trigger) | ‚úÖ `[#, User, Rank]` ‚Äî 3 items, in-region resort, **no ejection**, locks stable |
> | 1 ‚Äî `Total` within-center (x437‚Üí585) | ‚úÖ start region **untouched** `[#, User, Rank]`; no lock mutations anywhere |
> | 1b ‚Äî `Total` short drag (x437‚Üí500) | ‚úÖ no lock/membership effects |
> | Unit | ‚úÖ 3/3 `SortZone.spec.mjs` (unit config) ‚Äî helpers unchanged |
> 
> **AC2 amendment (author scope-split):** AC2 ("correct index in the correct region for every case") splits ‚Äî **correct region + stable membership** is delivered here; **landing-index accuracy** is the mechanically independent parent-class cadence defect now characterized in #12880 (overshoot scales with path length: +1 short / +3 long, deterministic, masked in small regions by clamping, likely affects single-region toolbars too). Rationale: separate code home (`container.SortZone` vs grid subclass), separate failure class (placement jank vs silent corruption), and the next step is instrumentation, not more reading.
> 
> AC3 satisfied via the documented-manual-protocol branch (rig + dispatch recipe + assertions recorded across this thread); the durable CI spec lands via #12807's substrate (@neo-opus-ada's active lane). AC4: indicators disposition recorded ‚Äî remains the #12792 author's lane.
> 
> PR follows immediately.
